data.title: Pfizer’s bladder cancer treatment achieves main goal in late-stage trial
data.description:
• Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) said on Friday it achieved main goal in late-stage trial evaluating sasanlimab in combination with Bacillus Calmette-Guérin as induction therapy with or without maintenance in patients with BCG-naïve, high-risk non-muscle invasive bladder cancer.
  • The study demonstrated a clinically meaningful and statistically significant improvement with sasanlimab in combination with BCG as compared to BCG alone.
  • The overall safety profile of sasanlimab in combination with BCG was generally consistent with the known profile of BCG and data reported from clinical trials with sasanlimab.
  • The profile of sasanlimab was also generally consistent with the reported safety profile of PD-1 inhibitors.
  • Sasanlimab also continues to be investigated in combination with Pfizer’s antibody drug conjugate portfolio in advanced solid tumors.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Net Lease Office Properties sells five sites for $43M, pays off secured mortgage
data.description:
Net Lease Office Properties (NYSE:NLOP) sold five office properties in November and December for gross proceeds of $43.3M, the REIT said on Friday.

Net proceeds were used to repay ~$30M of J.P. Morgan's senior secured mortgage and ~$5M on its mezzanine loans. Along with ~$23M of funds from other sources, NLOP paid the the senior secured mortgage in full and has an outstanding balance of ~$61M on its mezzanine loan as of Dec. 31, 2024.

Office property sales during Q4 brought total gross proceeds from dispositions completed in 2024 to ~$364M, including occupied sales of $319M at a weighted-average disposition cap rate of ~10.5%.

As of Dec. 31, 2024, Net Lease Office Properties (NYSE:NLOP) owned 39 office properties, comprising 37 properties in the U.S. and two in Europe.

Shares in Net Lease Office (NLOP) slipped 1.3% in Friday premarket trading.

data.title: EnviroGold Global upsizes its unit offering to up to C$4M
data.description:
• EnviroGold Global (OTC:ESGLF) has increased the size of its previously announced non-brokered private placement financing due to increased investor interest, to raise up to C$4M of gross proceeds, consisting of up to 4,000 units issued at a price of C$1,000/Unit.
  • Each Unit will consist of C$1,000 principal amounts of unsecured convertible notes and 11,112 common share purchase warrants of the Co.
  • All other terms of the Financing remain as set out in the Co.’s December 19, 2024 news release.

data.title: Tilray Brands in charts: Cannabis and Beverage businesses show sequential growth in FQ2
Constellation Brands Non-GAAP EPS of $3.25 misses by $0.06, revenue of $2.46B misses by $70M
data.description:
• Constellation Brands (NYSE:STZ): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.25 misses by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $2.46B (-0.4% Y/Y) misses by $70M.
  • Updates fiscal 2025 reported EPS outlook to $3.90 - $4.30 and comparable EPS outlook to $13.40 - $13.80, including $668 million of shares repurchased through November 2024
  • Raises fiscal 2025 operating cash flow target to $2.9 - $3.1 billionand free cash flow projection to $1.6 - $1.8 billion

data.title: D-Wave Quantum expects fiscal year bookings to top $23M
data.description:
D-Wave Quantum (NYSE:QBTS) shares popped 3.4% premarket on Friday after the quantum computing firm said its fiscal year 2024 bookings will likely top $23M, representing a growth of ~120% over fiscal year 2023 bookings.

The company expects Q4 bookings of at least $18M, an increase of ~500% over prior year fiscal 2023 Q4 bookings of $3M. It ended fiscal year 2024 with a record cash position of ~$178M in cash.

In addition, D-Wave (NYSE:QBTS) reported the first-ever customer purchase of its D-Wave Advantage annealing quantum computing system – said to be the world’s largest quantum computer with more than 5,000 qubits and 15-way connectivity.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: WBA surpasses FQ1 expectations, reaffirms FY2025 profit outlook
Lantronix names Brent Stringham as CFO
data.description:
• Lantronix (NASDAQ:LTRX) said it has appointed Brent Stringham as its chief financial officer.
  • Stringham has been a financial leader with the company since 2012 and had previously served as the firm's interim finance chief and chief accounting officer.

data.title: Olive Resource Capital estimates NAV per share at C$0.064 at December end
data.description:
• Olive Resource Capital (OTCPK:XTERF) said its unaudited net asset value per share is estimated at C$0.064 per share for December 31.
  • At the end of December, the firm's price per share was C$0.025.

data.title: Wayfair decides to exit the German market due to challenging conditions
data.description:
Wayfair Inc. (NYSE:W) announced on Friday its decision to exit the German market. The online retailer said the pullout from Germany will be effective immediately.

"Since Covid, and in particular over the past year, we have made meaningful progress in Germany, thanks to the work of many of you directly involved in the market. However, despite these efforts, scaling our market share and improving our unit economics in the German market has proven challenging due to factors such as the weak macroeconomic conditions for our category in Germany, the lower maturity of our offering, our current brand awareness, and our limited scale," read part of a letter sent to Wayfair (NYSE:W) employees in Germany.

The restructuring by Wayfair (W) in Germany is expected to result in a workforce restructuring impacting approximately 730 employees, although the company expects approximately half of those positions to relocate to other corporate offices.

As a result of the restructuring, Wayfair (W) estimates that it will incur aggregate charges of approximately $102 million to $111 million, consisting of approximately $40 million to $44 million in cash employee-related costs, and approximately $62 million to $67 million of other primarily non-cash charges, including charges related to facility closures and other wind-down activities.

The majority of the cash payments by Wayfair (W) are expected to be made over the next 12 months. While the company continues to evaluate the timing of non-cash charges, it anticipates that such charges will be incurred across the fourth quarter of 2024 and the first quarter of 2025.

Looking ahead, cost savings from the restructuring in Germany are expected to be largely reinvested across Wayfair's (W) core initiatives and remaining international markets over the balance of 2025.

Shares of Wayfair (W) were up 0.85% in premarket trading on Friday.
